name: Alfambra Valley — circular route via Cerro de Castelfrío
name_original: Valle de Alfambra — circular por el Cerro de Castelfrío
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Alfambra, located in the province of Teruel, Spain, offer diverse landscapes for cycling. The region features the Alfambra River valley, providing routes with minimal elevation changes. It is also situated within the broader mountainous terrain of Teruel, which includes the Sierra de Gúdar and Macizo de Javalambre ranges. Additionally, some routes showcase unique desert-like landscapes, adding variety to the natural scenery. Best mountain bike trails around Alfambra The most popular mountain bike trail is Alfambra Valley — circular route via Cerro de Castelfrío, a 34.2 miles (55.0 km) trail that takes 5 hours 12 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the Alfambra Valley and ascends Cerro de Castelfrío. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Baronía de Escriche — circular desde Corbalán, a moderate 16.3 miles (26.2 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the area of La Baronía de Escriche. Local mountain bikers also love the Desert landscape in Teruel – Teruel loop from Teruel, a 42.5 miles (68.4 km) trail leading through unique desert-like landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 46 minutes. Mountain biking around Alfambra is defined by the scenic Alfambra River valley, the mountainous terrain of Teruel, and access to the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes to challenging ascents. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 30 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Alfambra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Alfambra?
A: The region offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find routes along the gentle &lt;p&gt;Alfambra River valley with minimal elevation changes, as well as more challenging ascents in the mountainous areas of Teruel, including the Sierra de Gúdar and Macizo de Javalambre ranges. Some trails also feature unique desert-like landscapes, providing a varied riding experience.&lt;/p&gt;

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Alfambra?
A: While the guide primarily features moderate to difficult routes, the &lt;p&gt;Alfambra River valley offers sections with gentle terrain and minimal elevation changes, which can be suitable for less experienced riders. Additionally, the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, accessible from the broader region, is known for its generally downhill and car-free path, making it very accessible for various skill levels.&lt;/p&gt;

Q: What are some of the more challenging mountain bike routes near Alfambra?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995625479/alfambra-valley-circular-route-via-cerro-de-castelfrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfambra Valley — circular route via Cerro de Castelfrío&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34.2-mile (55.0 km) trail with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14710737" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desert landscape in Teruel – Teruel loop from Teruel&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 42.5 miles (68.4 km) through unique terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Alfambra?
A: The mountainous terrain of Teruel, where Alfambra is located, can experience varied weather. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for mountain biking. Summers can be hot, especially in areas with desert-like landscapes, while winters in the Sierra de Gúdar and Macizo de Javalambre ranges may bring colder conditions and snow at higher elevations. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike options around Alfambra?
A: While specific family-designated routes are not detailed in the guide, the &lt;p&gt;Alfambra River valley provides gentler sections that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous ride. The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros, a car-free greenway in the broader region, is also an excellent option for families due to its safety and accessibility.&lt;/p&gt;

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Alfambra?
A: Many natural trails in Spain, especially in rural and mountainous areas, are generally dog-friendly, provided dogs are kept under control. However, it's always best to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the ride and bring plenty of water for both of you.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several points of interest. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus of Alfambra&lt;/a&gt;. For scenic views, look out for mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5095753" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Cabigordo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2978665" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;mountain pass from Cedrillas to Ababuj&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2978663" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;dirt track towards the hill of Castelfrío&lt;/a&gt; also offers a glimpse into the natural surroundings.

Q: Is there public transport or parking available near the trailheads in Alfambra?
A: Information on specific public transport links directly to trailheads is limited. However, Alfambra is a town in Teruel province, and you can typically find parking within or near the town center. For routes starting further afield, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005425099/la-baronia-de-escriche-circular-desde-corbalan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Baronía de Escriche — circular desde Corbalán&lt;/a&gt;, you would likely find parking in the starting village of Corbalán.

Q: What is the Vía Verde de Ojos Negros and how does it relate to mountain biking in Alfambra?
A: The Vía Verde de Ojos Negros is Spain's longest greenway, a former mining railway line converted into a cycling and hiking path. While not directly in Alfambra, it's a major draw in the broader Teruel region. It offers a unique, generally downhill cycling experience from the interior towards the coast, traversing varied landscapes. It's well-regarded for its safety and accessibility, making it a popular choice for mountain bikers in the area. You can learn more about it on &lt;a href="https://es.wikipedia.org/wiki/V%C3%ADa_verde_de_Ojos_Negros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Wikipedia&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in the Alfambra area?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes available. A challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995625479/alfambra-valley-circular-route-via-cerro-de-castelfrio" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alfambra Valley — circular route via Cerro de Castelfrío&lt;/a&gt;, which is 34.2 miles (55.0 km) long. For a moderate ride, consider &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005425099/la-baronia-de-escriche-circular-desde-corbalan" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Baronía de Escriche — circular desde Corbalán&lt;/a&gt;, spanning 16.3 miles (26.2 km).

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails around Alfambra?
A: Mountain bikers exploring Alfambra often appreciate the region's diverse landscapes, from the serene Alfambra River valley to the more rugged mountainous terrain of Teruel. The variety of routes, including those with unique desert-like views, provides options for different skill levels and ensures a varied and engaging riding experience.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking on trails in Alfambra?
A: Generally, permits are not required for mountain biking on public trails in the Alfambra area. However, it's always advisable to respect private property, follow local signage, and adhere to any specific regulations that might apply to protected natural areas or private estates you may encounter.
tips: Alfambra is a town and municipality in the province of Teruel where we highlight the Christ of Alfambra, the Castle of the same name, the beetroot museum or the climb to the Monument to the Sacred Heart of Jesus with impressive views of the town and the valley.
Enjoy the fantastic views of the valleys and mountains in the 'Empty Mountains' area. There is a large road surface and interesting history at the location.
